flashcards.front: What is the main difference between Vector and ArrayList?
flashcards.back: Vector is synchronized (thread-safe) while ArrayList is not. Vector has legacy methods and is generally slower due to synchronization overhead.
Vector, Stack, Hashtable, Properties, Dictionary (any three)

flashcards.front: What interface preceded Iterator for collection traversal?
flashcards.back: Enumeration interface, with methods hasMoreElements() and nextElement().

flashcards.front: Why might someone still use Hashtable instead of HashMap?
flashcards.back: When thread safety is needed without external synchronization, and when null keys/values should be prohibited.

flashcards.front: What is the modern replacement for Stack class?
flashcards.back: ArrayDeque class implements the Deque interface and provides better performance for stack operations.
